questions.q: Cisco Umbrella blocks threats at the DNS layer — before a TCP/UDP connection is ever established.
Which IP addresses are Umbrella's primary DNS resolvers?
Umbrella requires TLS decryption to block encrypted HTTPS C2 traffic.
Cisco acquired OpenDNS (now Umbrella) for approximately $635 million in which year?
In the October 2020 Trickbot takedown scenario, which threat actor operated the Trickbot C2 infrastructure?
DNS tunneling encodes data in subdomain labels and works through strict firewalls because UDP port 53 is rarely blocked.
A normal user generates roughly 2,000 DNS queries per day — about the same as a DNS tunnel.
Which tool fingerprint was identified in the Singapore bank DNS tunnel scenario?
OilRig (APT34) used which type of DNS record primarily for its exfiltration tunnels?
Blocking a tunnel domain at the NXDOMAIN level is more robust than filtering by subdomain label length.
DGA malware generates hundreds of domains per day, but the attacker only needs to register one or two to maintain C2 access.
Conficker, the first widespread DGA botnet, generated approximately how many domains per day?
Blocking a single DGA-generated domain is sufficient to stop a DGA-based botnet.
In the NHS hospital scenario, which ransomware payload was Emotet attempting to deliver?
Umbrella's ML-based DGA detection flags NXDOMAIN storms combined with high-entropy (machine-generated looking) domain names.
In single-flux fast flux, the domain name itself rotates every few minutes along with the IP addresses.
Double-flux extends single-flux by also rotating which DNS record type in addition to A records?
IP-based blocklists are an effective long-term defense against fast flux networks.
Umbrella defeats fast flux by blocking at the domain-name level, making IP rotation irrelevant.
In the trading firm scenario, how many unique IP addresses did the flux-c2.net domain resolve to over 24 hours?
DNS rebinding works by changing a domain's DNS record to a private IP after the TTL expires, tricking the browser into treating the internal resource as same-origin.
Umbrella detects DNS rebinding by flagging public domains that return RFC-1918 private IP addresses in their DNS responses.
After a successful DNS rebind, the browser blocks the internal resource request because it detects a cross-origin violation.
A DNS rebinding attack requires the attacker to control the authoritative nameserver for the malicious domain.
In the IoT lab scenario, what type of device was exposed at 192.168.10.1?
IDN homograph attacks use Unicode characters that appear visually identical to ASCII letters — making the malicious domain undetectable to the naked eye in older browsers.
A TLS certificate from Let's Encrypt on a lookalike domain is a reliable trust indicator confirming the site is legitimate.
Umbrella's lookalike detection can only catch exact-match domains — it cannot identify one-character typosquats.
In the Pentagon contractor scenario, what character substitution made the lookalike domain hard to spot?
options: Zero ('0') replacing the letter 'a' at position 8
Cyrillic 'с' replacing the Latin 'c' at position 1

questions.q: NOBELIUM (APT29) is attributed to which nation-state intelligence service?
In Umbrella's policy hierarchy, a custom allow-list entry overrides security category blocks such as Malware and C2.
Which type of allow-list entry is safer and follows Umbrella best practices?
options: *.update-service.io with no expiry date applied globally
pkg.update-service.io with an expiry date scoped to the WORKSTATIONS group

questions.q: The rogue allow-list rule (RULE-2023-0092) in the school district scenario was added by an account that was still active at the time of the incident.
LockBit operators exploited the wildcard allow-list entry by registering a subdomain under the permitted wildcard domain.
How much did the school district LockBit attack ultimately cost in ransom and recovery?
When malware uses DoH (DNS over HTTPS) with a hardcoded resolver IP like 1.1.1.1:443, Cisco Umbrella's standard DNS resolver sees and blocks the queries.
Godlua (2019) was the first malware confirmed to use DoH for C2 evasion. It targeted which type of systems?
options: Linux and Windows servers across Asia-Pacific

questions.q: Umbrella's roaming client blocks all DoH connections, including those going to Umbrella's own cloud DoH endpoint (208.67.222.222:443).
In the Tokyo scenario, the suspicious process making DoH connections was named svchost32.exe — a legitimate Windows system process.
The decoded DoH payload in the Tokyo scenario revealed a DNS query for which domain?
Passive DNS records historical DNS resolutions, allowing analysts to discover domains that shared IPs with known C2 servers.
Talos pushes confirmed malicious domains to Umbrella globally within approximately 4 hours of analyst confirmation.
In the Scattered Spider (UNC3944) campaign, Talos blocked campaign infrastructure after the majority of attacks had already succeeded.
How many domains were identified in the Scattered Spider 2023 campaign infrastructure via passive DNS pivoting?
In the scenario, all 47 Scattered Spider campaign domains were registered at the same registrar within a few days using cryptocurrency payment.
DNS logs can identify compromised hosts — including unmanaged OT/SCADA devices — faster than EDR telemetry alone.
VOLT TYPHOON (Chinese APT) primarily used custom malware with a heavy exploit footprint for persistence in US critical infrastructure.
DNS containment blocks C2 domains for all infected hosts simultaneously, including unmanaged OT and SCADA devices.
In the ERCOT scenario, how many OT hosts were found querying the VOLT TYPHOON C2 domain?
CISA's Critical Infrastructure Protection Guidelines (CPG 2.S) recommend DNS-layer security as a baseline control for critical infrastructure operators.
